qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Template Injection via unsafe file include functions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSTI via template include functions in modules/caddyhttp/templates, while the advisory describes a Unicode case-folding path splitting vulnerability in the FastCGI transport module (modules/caddyhttp/fastcgi). These are completely different modules, different vulnerability types, and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a FastCGI path splitting vulnerability caused by Unicode case-folding length expansion in modules/caddyhttp/fastcgi, while the only qualifying finding describes SSTI via template include functions in modules/caddyhttp/templates. These are different modules, different vulnerability types (path confusion vs. SSTI), and different root causes.
evidence: Advisory describes vulnerability in github.com/caddyserver/caddy/modules/caddyhttp/fastcgi.Transport.splitPos() and buildEnv()
Advisory root cause: strings.ToLower() UTF-8 byte length expansion causing incorrect split_path index
Advisory impact: incorrect SCRIPT_NAME/SCRIPT_FILENAME/PATH_INFO leading to path confusion and potential PHP execution
Finding describes vulnerability in modules/caddyhttp/templates/tplcontext.go (funcInclude, funcReadFile, funcImport)
Finding root cause: missing path validation in readFileToBuffer() allowing path traversal
Finding impact: SSTI via arbitrary file read and template execution
No overlap in vulnerable functions, modules, or root cause mechanisms
